scope: Python source + config + external dependencies (.env, requirements)

vulnerabilities.description: Live DeepSeek API key hardcoded in plaintext: sk-96c8c56a71f6...
Live Google API key hardcoded in plaintext: AIzaSyBWRCPmYpH...
Live Alpha Vantage API key hardcoded in plaintext: FQZDH795B6P1JXSU
Database credentials hardcoded in connection string: mysql+pymysql://stockdb:stockdb123@localhost/stockdb
4 live API keys in .env file (FINANCIAL_DATASETS_API_KEY, ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY) — NOT gitignored in submodule

7 live API/trading keys in .env including Alpaca trading credentials (ALPACA_API_KEY, ALPACA_API_SECRET, ALPACA_BASE_URL) plus ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY — NOT gitignored in submodule
XSS: st.markdown() with unsafe_allow_html=True used for sidebar branding — establishes unsafe pattern
XSS: Dynamic stock symbols/names from DB embedded directly into HTML via st.markdown(unsafe_allow_html=True) — if DB contains malicious data, XSS is exploitable
XSS: Financial data (prices, market cap, PE ratios) rendered via st.markdown(unsafe_allow_html=True) with f-string interpolation from DB — data source compromise = XSS
XSS: Company names and sector/industry data from DB rendered via unsafe_allow_html=True
API keys from config.yaml exported to os.environ — leaks keys into process environment readable by any subprocess or dependency
No authentication on Streamlit dashboard — entire trading platform accessible without login
Database URL loaded from config.yaml with no encrypted secrets support — plaintext credentials in config file
No version pinning on critical dependencies (langgraph, langchain-*, etc.) — transitive dependency vulnerabilities possible. No pip-audit or safety check in CI/CD
XGBoost model files loaded from disk via pickle — no integrity verification (checksum/signature). Malicious model file = code execution via pickle deserialization
sys.path manipulation at module level — if external/ directories contain malicious packages, they shadow stdlib/AIStock modules
get_sectors() constructs raw SQL with f-string for table name — only hardcoded values, no user input, but fragile pattern
ThreadPoolExecutor used without explicit process isolation — worker threads share process memory including os.environ with exported API keys
File path input via st.text_input with no path traversal validation — user could specify arbitrary paths

positiveFindings: SQLAlchemy ORM used for 95%+ of queries — parameterized by default, immune to SQL injection
Raw text() queries use bind parameters (:ids, :sid) correctly — no string concatenation with user input
Session lifecycle managed with try/finally — no connection leaks
TokenBucket rate limiter in fetcher/ prevents API abuse
yaml.safe_load() used instead of yaml.load() — no arbitrary code execution via YAML
No exec(), eval(), or subprocess with shell=True found in source
No pickle.load() with untrusted data found (model pickle is internally generated)
Deadlock retry with exponential backoff in ingestion pipeline
config.yaml is gitignored in root project (though submodule .env files are NOT)
Alpaca trading credentials use paper trading by default (ALPACA_PAPER=true)
recommendations: IMMEDIATE: Rotate all API keys exposed in config.yaml — DeepSeek (sk-96c8...), Google (AIzaSy...), Alpha Vantage (FQZDH...). Keys are compromised by being in this audit context.
IMMEDIATE: Rotate Alpaca trading API key and secret from external/TradingAgents/.env — trading credentials exposed.
IMMEDIATE: Rotate database password (stockdb123) and restrict DB access to localhost only.
CRITICAL: Move all secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g., .env file with python-dotenv, macOS Keychain, or AWS Secrets Manager). Never store secrets in config.yaml.
CRITICAL: Verify external/ai-hedge-fund/.env and external/TradingAgents/.env are gitignored in their respective submodules. Add to .gitignore if not.
HIGH: Replace all st.markdown(unsafe_allow_html=True) with safe alternatives — st.write(), st.metric(), or HTML-escaping wrapper for dynamic data. Use bleach sanitizer if HTML is required.
HIGH: Add authentication to Streamlit dashboard — streamlit-authenticator, OAuth proxy, or password gate with server-side secret comparison.
HIGH: Add input validation for all user-provided text fields. Validate symbols against regex (^[A-Z]{1,5}$). Validate paths with pathlib.Path.resolve() within allowed directories.
MEDIUM: Pin dependency versions in requirements.txt with exact versions (==). Run pip-audit or safety check in CI pipeline.
MEDIUM: Add model file integrity verification — SHA-256 hash on save, verify on load before pickle deserialization.
MEDIUM: Add CSP headers to Streamlit app and implement secret redaction in logging.
LOW: Replace raw SQL f-string in get_sectors() with parameterized query or whitelist table names.
LOW: Set up Bandit static analysis in CI pipeline (pip install bandit && bandit -r src/).
LOW: Add .env to .gitignore for all submodules and verify with git check-ignore.
